intro: Peschiera hystrix, also known as the rough-barked horse's hoof, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties.
